enonce.texte:
Instructions: Match the children's rights in Column A with their descriptions in Column B.

Column A

  • Right to education
  • Right to health
  • Right to protection
  • Right to food
  • Right to shelter

Column B

  • A. Every child should have a home.
  • B. Every child has the right to go to school.
  • C. Every child should be safe from harm.
  • D. Every child should have enough to eat.
  • E. Every child should have access to medical care.

questions.texte: 1. Match the rights with their correct descriptions.
questions.reponse:
Answers:
Right to education → B
Right to health → E
Right to protection → C
Right to food → D
Right to shelter → A

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below carefully and answer the questions that follow.

Every Child Has Rights

Every child in the world has rights. These rights are written in the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child. The first right is the right to education. Every child should go to school and learn. The second right is the right to health. Children should see a doctor when they are sick. They should also have clean water and good food. The third right is the right to protection. Children should be safe from violence and abuse. They should not be forced to work. They should live in a safe environment. These rights are very important. They help children grow and become happy adults.

questions.texte: 1. Identify the document that contains the rights of the child.
questions.reponse: Answer: The United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child.

questions.texte: 2. List three rights mentioned in the text.
questions.reponse: Answer: 1. Right to education. 2. Right to health. 3. Right to protection.

questions.texte: 3. Describe what the right to health means according to the text.
questions.reponse: Answer: The right to health means that children should see a doctor when they are sick and have clean water and good food.

questions.texte: 4. Explain why children's rights are important according to the text.
questions.reponse: Answer: Children's rights are important because they help children grow and become happy adults.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box.

Word Box: education, health, protection, food, shelter, violence, abuse, safe

questions.texte: 1. Children have the right to ________. They should go to school.
2. Every child needs ________. They should have enough to eat every day.
3. Children should be ________ from harm and danger.
4. The right to ________ means that children should be treated kindly and not hurt.
5. Children should not suffer from ________ or mistreatment.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the sentences with should or should not based on children's rights.

questions.texte: 1. Children ________ go to school.
2. Children ________ be forced to work.
3. Children ________ have access to clean water.
4. Children ________ live in a safe environment.
5. Children ________ be left alone without care.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Write a short paragraph (about 60-80 words) about children's rights. Use the prompts below to guide you.

  • Prompt 1: What are children's rights?
  • Prompt 2: Name three rights that are important to you.
  • Prompt 3: Why are these rights important?
  • Use at least 4 vocabulary words: education, health, protection, shelter, food, safe.

questions.texte: 1. Write a short paragraph about children's rights.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:
Children have many rights. The right to education is very important because it helps children learn and have a better future. The right to health is also important because children need to be healthy to grow and play. The right to protection is necessary so that children are safe from harm. Every child should be able to live in a safe home and have enough food to eat. These rights help children become happy and successful adults.

intitule: Exercise 6: Problem-Solving – Helping Children in Need

enonce.texte:
Scenario: In your community, there are some children who do not go to school. They work in the fields instead of studying. Some children also do not have enough food to eat. The village chief has asked you to help solve this problem. You have a budget of 200,000 CFA to help these children. Your task is to plan how to help them and answer the questions.

Possible Actions:

  • Provide school supplies (books, pens, bags) – 50,000 CFA
  • Pay school fees for 10 children – 100,000 CFA
  • Organize a daily feeding program – 60,000 CFA
  • Build a community library – 150,000 CFA
  • Organize awareness meetings with parents – 30,000 CFA
  • Hire a tutor for evening classes – 80,000 CFA
images: A group of children sitting in a classroom with books and pens, smiling and learning. A teacher is writing on a blackboard. The classroom is bright and welcoming.

questions.texte: 1. Calculate the total cost of all the possible actions listed.
2. Analyze the budget situation. Is the budget enough to cover all actions? If not, what changes would you suggest to make it work?
questions.reponse: Answer: The budget is not enough. The total is 470,000 CFA, which is 270,000 CFA more than the allocated budget. I would suggest: 1) Removing the library project (saving 150,000 CFA). 2) Reducing school fees to cover 5 children instead of 10 (saving 50,000 CFA). 3) Organizing parents to help with the feeding program (saving 30,000 CFA). This would bring the total to 180,000 CFA, which is within the budget.

questions.texte: 3. Propose a plan to help the children. Explain which actions you will take and why.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:
I propose the following plan:
1) Pay school fees for 5 children – 50,000 CFA. This will send 5 children to school.
2) Provide school supplies – 50,000 CFA. This will give materials to the children.
3) Organize awareness meetings with parents – 30,000 CFA. This will educate parents about the importance of education.
4) Organize a daily feeding program with help from parents – 30,000 CFA. This will provide food for the children.
Total: 160,000 CFA. This plan helps children go to school, have supplies, and get food.

questions.texte: 4. Justify why it is important to include awareness meetings with parents in your plan.
questions.reponse: Answer: Awareness meetings with parents are important because many children do not go to school because their parents do not understand the value of education. These meetings can teach parents about children's rights and the benefits of sending their children to school. When parents are educated, they are more likely to support their children's education. This creates a long-term solution to the problem.
